web_text: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to the chosen ones who are living as foreigners in the Dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia, according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, in sanctification of the Spirit, that you may obey Jesus Christ and be sprinkled with his blood: Grace to you and peace be multiplied. Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who according to his great mercy caused us to be born again to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead, to an incorruptible and undefiled inheritance that doesn’t fade away, reserved in Heaven for you,
slang_text: It's Peter here, an apostle of Jesus Christ, writin' to the chosen ones scattered as foreigners across Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia, all accordin' to God the Father's foreknowledge, made holy by the Spirit, so you'd obey Jesus Christ and get sprinkled with His blood: Grace and peace to you, pilin' up more and more, for real. Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who, outta His totally massive mercy, gave us a brand-new birth into a livin' hope through Jesus Christ's resurrection from the dead, into an inheritance that can't rot, can't get stained, can't ever fade away — righteous and kept safe for you up in heaven,

web_text: who by the power of God are guarded through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in the last time. Wherein you greatly rejoice, though now for a little while, if need be, you have been put to grief in various trials, that the proof of your faith, which is more precious than gold that perishes even though it is tested by fire, may be found to result in praise, glory, and honor at the revelation of Jesus Christ— whom not having known you love; in whom, though now you don’t see him, yet believing, you rejoice greatly with joy unspeakable and full of glory—
slang_text: who're guarded by God's own power through faith, for a salvation that's all set to get revealed at the very end. That's why you're stoked about it, even though right now you're catchin' grief through all kinds of trials for a little while, if that's what it takes — so the proof of your faith, way more precious than gold that still rots even after gettin' tested by fire, can turn into praise, glory, and honor when Jesus Christ gets revealed. You love Him even though you've never laid eyes on Him; even now you can't see Him, but you believe, and you're stoked with a joy so epic there's no words for it, loaded up with glory —

web_text: receiving the result of your faith, the salvation of your souls. Concerning this salvation, the prophets sought and searched diligently, who prophesied of the grace that would come to you, searching for who or what kind of time the Spirit of Christ, which was in them, pointed to, when he predicted the sufferings of Christ, and the glories that would follow them. To them it was revealed, that not to themselves, but to you, they ministered these things, which now have been announced to you through those who preached the Good News to you by the Holy Spirit sent out from heaven; which things angels desire to look into.
slang_text: scorin' the payoff of your faith — the salvation of your souls. This salvation's exactly what the prophets went diggin' and searchin' hard for, the ones who prophesied about the grace that was gonna come your way, tryin' to figure out who or what time Christ's own Spirit inside 'em was pointin' to, when He predicted Christ's sufferin' and the glory that'd follow it. It got revealed to 'em that they weren't servin' themselves with this stuff, they were servin' you, and now it's finally gotten announced to you through the folks who preached the Good News, backed by the Holy Spirit sent straight from heaven — stuff even angels are dyin' to get a peek at.

web_text: Therefore prepare your minds for action, be sober, and set your hope fully on the grace that will be brought to you at the revelation of Jesus Christ— as children of obedience, not conforming yourselves according to your former lusts as in your ignorance, but just as he who called you is holy, you yourselves also be holy in all of your behavior; because it is written, “You shall be holy; for I am holy.”
slang_text: So gear up your minds for action, no foolin', stay level-headed, and pin all your hope on the grace that's totally comin' your way when Jesus Christ gets revealed — actin' like obedient kids, not slippin' back into the cravings you used to chase when you were clueless. Instead, just like the One who called you is holy, you be holy too, in everything you do, fer sure, 'cause it's written: "You're gonna be holy, 'cause I'm holy."

web_text: If you call on him as Father, who without respect of persons judges according to each man’s work, pass the time of your living as foreigners here in reverent fear: knowing that you were redeemed, not with corruptible things, with silver or gold, from the useless way of life handed down from your fathers, but with precious blood, as of a lamb without blemish or spot, the blood of Christ; who was foreknown indeed before the foundation of the world, but was revealed in this last age for your sake,
slang_text: If you're callin' on Him as Father, the One who judges without playin' favorites, matchin' each person up with their own deeds, then spend your time livin' here like a foreigner, with reverent fear: knowin' you got redeemed, not with rottin' stuff like silver or gold, from that useless, bogus way of life your ancestors handed down, but with precious blood — like a spotless, flawless lamb — the blood of Christ. He was already known ahead of time, way before the world's foundation, no lie, but got revealed in these last days for your sake,

web_text: who through him are believers in God, who raised him from the dead, and gave him glory; so that your faith and hope might be in God. Seeing you have purified your souls in your obedience to the truth through the Spirit in sincere brotherly affection, love one another from the heart fervently: having been born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, through the word of God, which lives and remains forever. For, “All flesh is like grass, and all of man’s glory like the flower in the grass. The grass withers, and its flower falls;
slang_text: you who believe in God through Him, who raised Him from the dead and glorified Him, so your faith and hope would rest in God, for real. Since you've purified your souls by obeyin' the truth through the Spirit, into genuine brotherly love, love each other hard, straight from the heart, no half-steppin' — you've been born again, not from seed that rots, but from seed that never rots, through God's word, which lives and sticks around forever, righteous and true. 'Cause "all flesh is like grass, and all of man's glory's like the flower in the grass. The grass withers, and its flower drops off;

web_text: but the Lord’s word endures forever.” This is the word of Good News which was preached to you.
slang_text: but the Lord's word sticks around forever, fer sure." And that's the Good News that got preached to you, straight up.

web_text: Putting away therefore all wickedness, all deceit, hypocrisies, envies, and all evil speaking, as newborn babies, long for the pure milk of the Word, that with it you may grow, if indeed you have tasted that the Lord is gracious: coming to him, a living stone, rejected indeed by men, but chosen by God, precious.
slang_text: So ditch every bit of wickedness, deceit, two-faced acts, jealousy, and trash-talkin', and like a newborn baby, crave the pure milk of the Word, so it helps you grow big and strong — if you've actually tasted how righteous the Lord is. Come to Him, the livin' stone, rejected by people, sure, but picked out by God and totally precious.

web_text: You also, as living stones, are built up as a spiritual house, to be a holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices, acceptable to God through Jesus Christ. Because it is contained in Scripture, “Behold, I lay in Zion a chief cornerstone, chosen, and precious: He who believes in him will not be disappointed.” For you who believe therefore is the honor, but for those who are disobedient, “The stone which the builders rejected, has become the chief cornerstone,” and, “a stone of stumbling, and a rock of offense.” For they stumble at the word, being disobedient, to which also they were appointed.
slang_text: You too, like livin' stones, are gettin' built up into a spiritual house, a holy priesthood, offerin' up spiritual sacrifices God's totally into, through Jesus Christ. 'Cause Scripture says it plain: "Check it out, I'm layin' a cornerstone in Zion, picked out and precious — whoever believes in Him won't come up bogus." So for you believers, that's an honor, but for the disobedient crowd, "The stone the builders rejected has become the main cornerstone," and, "a stumblin' stone, a rock that trips people up." They trip over the word 'cause they're disobedient, which is exactly what they were headed for anyway.

web_text: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for God’s own possession, that you may proclaim the excellence of him who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light: who in time past were no people, but now are God’s people, who had not obtained mercy, but now have obtained mercy. Beloved, I beg you as foreigners and pilgrims, to abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul; having good behavior among the nations, so in that of which they speak against you as evildoers, they may by your good works, which they see, glorify God in the day of visitation.
slang_text: But you're a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people that belongs to God — so you can proclaim how epic He is, the One who called you outta darkness into His crazy-bright light. You used to be nobody, but now you're God's people; you never got mercy, but now you got mercy. Beloved, I'm beggin' you, as foreigners just passin' through, steer clear of fleshly cravings that war against your soul; live right in front of everybody, so even when they trash-talk you as troublemakers, they'll see your good deeds and end up glorifyin' God when He shows up.

web_text: Therefore subject yourselves to every ordinance of man for the Lord’s sake: whether to the king, as supreme; or to governors, as sent by him for vengeance on evildoers and for praise to those who do well. For this is the will of God, that by well-doing you should put to silence the ignorance of foolish men: as free, and not using your freedom for a cloak of wickedness, but as bondservants of God.
slang_text: So yield to every human authority, for the Lord's sake — whether it's the king, top of the chain, or governors, sent by him to punish evildoers and give props to folks doin' right. 'Cause this is what God wants: doin' good shuts up the ignorant babblin' of foolish people, no doubt. Live free, but don't use that freedom as a cover for wickedness — live like servants of God instead, fer sure.

web_text: Honor all men. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honor the king. Servants, be in subjection to your masters with all fear; not only to the good and gentle, but also to the wicked. For it is commendable if someone endures pain, suffering unjustly, because of conscience toward God. For what glory is it if, when you sin, you patiently endure beating? But if, when you do well, you patiently endure suffering, this is commendable with God.
slang_text: Honor everybody. Love the family of believers. Fear God. Honor the king. Servants, yield to your masters with total respect, not just the good, chill ones, but the harsh ones too. 'Cause it's commendable if somebody puts up with pain, sufferin' unfairly, 'cause their conscience is set on God. What's so great about patiently takin' a beatin' when you actually messed up, no duh? But if you patiently suffer while doin' right, that's straight-up commendable in God's eyes.

web_text: For to this you were called, because Christ also suffered for us, leaving you an example, that you should follow his steps, who did not sin, “neither was deceit found in his mouth.” Who, when he was cursed, didn’t curse back. When he suffered, didn’t threaten, but committed himself to him who judges righteously. He himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we, having died to sins, might live to righteousness; by whose stripes you were healed.
slang_text: 'Cause that's exactly what you got called to — Christ suffered for us too, leavin' you an example so you'd follow in His footsteps. He never sinned, "and no deceit ever got found in His mouth." When people cursed Him, He didn't curse back, no way. When He suffered, He didn't threaten nobody, just handed Himself over to the One who judges fair and square. He carried our sins in His own body, up on the tree, so we'd die to sin and live for righteousness — and it's His stripes that healed you, for real.

web_text: For you were going astray like sheep; but now have returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.
slang_text: You used to wander off like sheep, way lost, but now you've come back to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.

web_text: In the same way, wives, be in subjection to your own husbands; so that, even if any don’t obey the Word, they may be won by the behavior of their wives without a word; seeing your pure behavior in fear. Let your beauty be not just the outward adorning of braiding the hair, and of wearing jewels of gold, or of putting on fine clothing; but in the hidden person of the heart, in the incorruptible adornment of a gentle and quiet spirit, which is in the sight of God very precious.
slang_text: Same deal, wives, yield to your own husbands, so even if some of 'em won't obey the Word, they might get won over just by watchin' their wives live right, no words needed at all — seein' your pure behavior, done with respect. Let your beauty not be just the outside stuff, braidin' your hair, wearin' gold jewelry, or puttin' on fancy threads — but the hidden person inside your heart, the kind of beauty that never fades, a gentle and quiet spirit, which is totally precious in God's eyes.

web_text: For this is how the holy women before, who hoped in God also adorned themselves, being in subjection to their own husbands: as Sarah obeyed Abraham, calling him lord, whose children you now are, if you do well, and are not put in fear by any terror. You husbands, in the same way, live with your wives according to knowledge, giving honor to the woman, as to the weaker vessel, as being also joint heirs of the grace of life; that your prayers may not be hindered. Finally, be all like-minded, compassionate, loving as brothers, tender hearted, courteous,
slang_text: 'Cause that's exactly how the holy women of the past, who put their hope in God, decked themselves out — yieldin' to their own husbands, same as Sarah obeyed Abraham, callin' him lord. You're her kids now, fer sure, long as you do right and don't get spooked by nothin'. You husbands, same deal, live with your wives with real understandin', honorin' 'em as the more delicate partner, since you're both heirs together of the grace of life — so your prayers don't get blocked. Finally, everybody stay on the same page, compassionate, lovin' each other like family, tender-hearted, courteous,

web_text: not rendering evil for evil, or insult for insult; but instead blessing; knowing that to this were you called, that you may inherit a blessing. For, “He who would love life, and see good days, let him keep his tongue from evil, and his lips from speaking deceit. Let him turn away from evil, and do good. Let him seek peace, and pursue it. For the eyes of the Lord are on the righteous, and his ears open to their prayer; but the face of the Lord is against those who do evil.”
slang_text: not payin' back evil for evil, or insult for insult — bless folks instead, no doubt, knowin' that's exactly what you got called to, so you'd inherit a blessin'. 'Cause "whoever wants to love life and see good days, let him keep his tongue off evil, keep his lips from lyin'. Let him turn away from evil and do good, for real. Let him chase peace, hunt it down, no joke. 'Cause the Lord's eyes are on the righteous, and His ears are open to their prayers — but the Lord's face is set against whoever does evil."

web_text: Now who is he who will harm you, if you become imitators of that which is good? But even if you should suffer for righteousness’ sake, you are blessed. “Don’t fear what they fear, neither be troubled.” But sanctify the Lord God in your hearts; and always be ready to give an answer to everyone who asks you a reason concerning the hope that is in you, with humility and fear: having a good conscience; that, while you are spoken against as evildoers, they may be disappointed who curse your good way of life in Christ.
slang_text: Now who's actually gonna hurt you if you're out here copyin' whatever's good? But even if you gotta suffer for doin' right, you're blessed. "Don't fear what they fear, don't get rattled." Keep the Lord God holy in your hearts, and always stay ready to answer anybody who asks why you got this hope in you, doin' it humble and respectful, keepin' a clean conscience — so when they trash-talk you as troublemakers, the ones cursin' your good life in Christ end up lookin' bogus instead.

web_text: For it is better, if it is God’s will, that you suffer for doing well than for doing evil. Because Christ also suffered for sins once, the righteous for the unrighteous, that he might bring you to God; being put to death in the flesh, but made alive in the spirit; in which he also went and preached to the spirits in prison, who before were disobedient, when God waited patiently in the days of Noah, while the ship was being built. In it, few, that is, eight souls, were saved through water.
slang_text: 'Cause it's way better, if it's God's will, to suffer for doin' right than for doin' wrong, no lie. Christ suffered for sins once too, the righteous One for the unrighteous, so He could bring you to God — put to death in the body, but made alive in the spirit, for real. In that spirit, He went and preached to the spirits locked up, the ones who were disobedient way back when God patiently waited durin' Noah's day, while the ship was gettin' built. Only a few, eight souls, got saved through that water.

web_text: This is a symbol of baptism, which now saves you—not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God, through the resurrection of Jesus Christ, who is at the right hand of God, having gone into heaven, angels and authorities and powers being made subject to him.
slang_text: That's a picture of baptism, which saves you now too, fer sure — not washin' off physical grime, but a clean conscience answerin' to God, through Jesus Christ's resurrection. He's up at God's right hand now, gone into heaven, with angels, authorities, and powers all put under His control, no doubt about it.

web_text: Therefore, since Christ suffered for us in the flesh, arm yourselves also with the same mind; for he who has suffered in the flesh has ceased from sin; that you no longer should live the rest of your time in the flesh for the lusts of men, but for the will of God. For we have spent enough of our past time doing the desire of the Gentiles, and having walked in lewdness, lusts, drunken binges, orgies, carousings, and abominable idolatries. They think it is strange that you don’t run with them into the same excess of riot, blaspheming:
slang_text: So since Christ suffered for us in His body, gear up with the same mindset — whoever's suffered in the body's already quit sinnin', so you don't gotta spend the rest of your life chasin' human cravings, but chasin' God's will instead. We already burned through plenty of time doin' what the Gentiles wanted, walkin' around in raunchy stuff, cravings, drunken binges, wild orgies, partyin', and grody idol worship. They think it's weird you won't run with 'em into all that excess anymore, and they trash-talk you for it:

web_text: who will give account to him who is ready to judge the living and the dead. For to this end the Good News was preached even to the dead, that they might be judged indeed as men in the flesh, but live as to God in the spirit. But the end of all things is near. Therefore be of sound mind, self-controlled, and sober in prayer. And above all things be earnest in your love among yourselves, for love covers a multitude of sins.
slang_text: but they're gonna answer to the One who's ready to judge the livin' and the dead. That's exactly why the Good News got preached even to the dead, so they'd be judged like everybody else in the body, but live for God in the spirit. But the end of everything's gettin' close, for real. So stay sound-minded, self-controlled, and sober about your prayers. And above all, stay fired up in your love for each other, no matter what, 'cause love covers a whole mountain of sins.

web_text: Be hospitable to one another without grumbling. As each has received a gift, employ it in serving one another, as good managers of the grace of God in its various forms. If anyone speaks, let it be as it were the very words of God. If anyone serves, let it be as of the strength which God supplies, that in all things God may be glorified through Jesus Christ, to whom belong the glory and the dominion forever and ever. Amen. Beloved, don’t be astonished at the fiery trial which has come upon you, to test you, as though a strange thing happened to you.
slang_text: Be hospitable to each other, no grumblin' about it. Whatever gift each of you got handed, use it to serve each other, like solid managers of God's grace in all its different flavors, no doubt. If anybody speaks, let it sound like God's own words. If anybody serves, let it run on the strength God supplies, so God gets glorified through Jesus Christ in everything — to whom belongs the glory and the power, forever and ever. Amen. Beloved, don't get shocked over the fiery trial that's hit you to test you, like it's some weird, freak thing happenin' to you outta nowhere.

web_text: But because you are partakers of Christ’s sufferings, rejoice; that at the revelation of his glory you also may rejoice with exceeding joy. If you are insulted for the name of Christ, you are blessed; because the Spirit of glory and of God rests on you. On their part he is blasphemed, but on your part he is glorified. For let none of you suffer as a murderer, or a thief, or an evil doer, or a meddler in other men’s matters. But if one of you suffers for being a Christian, let him not be ashamed; but let him glorify God in this matter.
slang_text: Instead, since you're sharin' in Christ's own sufferin', rejoice — so when His glory gets revealed, you'll rejoice big time, off the charts. If you get insulted for Christ's name, you're blessed, 'cause the Spirit of glory, the Spirit of God, rests right on you. On their side, He gets blasphemed, but on your side, He gets glorified. None of you oughta suffer as a murderer, thief, criminal, or nosy busybody in other folks' business. But if somebody suffers for bein' a Christian, don't be ashamed — glorify God over it instead.

web_text: For the time has come for judgment to begin with the household of God. If it begins first with us, what will happen to those who don’t obey the Good News of God? “If it is hard for the righteous to be saved, what will happen to the ungodly and the sinner?” Therefore let them also who suffer according to the will of God in doing good entrust their souls to him, as to a faithful Creator.
slang_text: 'Cause the time's come for judgment to start with God's own household, no lie. And if it's startin' with us first, what's gonna happen to the folks who won't obey God's Good News? "If it's rough for the righteous to get saved, what's gonna happen to the ungodly and the sinner?" So whoever's sufferin' accordin' to God's will while doin' good oughta hand their souls over to Him, fer sure, trustin' Him like a faithful Creator.

web_text: Therefore I exhort the elders among you, as a fellow elder, and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, and who will also share in the glory that will be revealed. Shepherd the flock of God which is among you, exercising the oversight, not under compulsion, but voluntarily, not for dishonest gain, but willingly; neither as lording it over those entrusted to you, but making yourselves examples to the flock. When the chief Shepherd is revealed, you will receive the crown of glory that doesn’t fade away.
slang_text: So I'm layin' it on the elders among you, straight up, as a fellow elder myself, and a witness to Christ's sufferin', and somebody who's gonna share in the glory that's comin' too. Shepherd God's flock that's under your care, keepin' watch over 'em — not 'cause you're forced to, but 'cause you want to, not chasin' dirty money, but doin' it willingly; not bossin' around the folks entrusted to you like some big shot, but bein' examples for the flock instead. When the chief Shepherd shows up, you're gonna score the crown of glory that never fades away, no lie.

web_text: Likewise, you younger ones, be subject to the elder. Yes, all of you clothe yourselves with humility, to subject yourselves to one another; for “God resists the proud, but gives grace to the humble.” Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that he may exalt you in due time; casting all your worries on him, because he cares for you. Be sober and self-controlled. Be watchful. Your adversary, the devil, walks around like a roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour.
slang_text: Same deal, you younger folks, yield to the elders. Yeah, everybody, suit up in humility, yieldin' to each other — 'cause "God fights the proud, but pours out grace on the humble." So humble yourselves under God's mighty hand, so He can lift you up right on time; toss all your worries His way, no joke, 'cause He cares about you. Stay sober, stay self-controlled. Stay alert, don't space out. Your enemy, the devil, prowls around like a roarin' lion, huntin' for somebody to devour.

web_text: Withstand him steadfast in your faith, knowing that your brothers who are in the world are undergoing the same sufferings. But may the God of all grace, who called you to his eternal glory by Christ Jesus, after you have suffered a little while, perfect, establish, strengthen, and settle you. To him be the glory and the power forever and ever. Amen. Through Silvanus, our faithful brother, as I consider him, I have written to you briefly, exhorting, and testifying that this is the true grace of God in which you stand.
slang_text: Stand your ground against him, solid as a rock in your faith, knowin' your brothers out there in the world are goin' through the same sufferin'. But may the God of all grace, who called you to His eternal glory through Christ Jesus, once you've suffered a little while, make you complete, lock you in solid, strengthen you, and settle you down for good. To Him be the glory and the power forever and ever. Amen. Through Silvanus, our faithful brother — that's how I see him, anyway — I've written you this short letter, encouragin' you and testifyin' fer sure that this is the real, genuine grace of God, and you're standin' right in it.

web_text: She who is in Babylon, chosen together with you, greets you; and so does Mark, my son. Greet one another with a kiss of love. Peace be to you all who are in Christ Jesus. Amen.
slang_text: The church over in Babylon, chosen right along with you, sends its greetin's, and so does Mark, my own kid, for real. Greet each other with a kiss of love. Peace be to all of you who're in Christ Jesus, no doubt. Amen.
